description_long: This is a mid-rise brick apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a stepped roofline, featuring a smaller upper section set back from the main facade. The exterior is clad in weathered red-brown brick, with large, evenly spaced windows arranged in vertical columns across all visible sides. Many of the windows are boarded up or have broken panes, giving the building a neglected, abandoned appearance. The flat roofs are surfaced with a rough, gray material and bordered by low parapet walls. The facade shows signs of age and wear, with some window frames rusted and paint peeling. There are no visible balconies or decorative architectural elements, and the entrance is not clearly visible from any angle.
description: A weathered, multi-story brick apartment building with large, boarded-up windows and a stepped flat roof, showing signs of neglect and age.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stepped roofline and the upper section with three large windows, as well as the main facade with rows of boarded and broken windows.
description_view_1: The upper section is prominent, and the side wall reveals more windows, some of which are rusted or boarded, emphasizing the building's dilapidated state.
description_view_2: The main facade is fully visible, showing the symmetrical arrangement of windows and the flat roof, with clear signs of weathering and damage.
description_view_3: This angle shows the side and front walls, with the stepped roof and the consistent window pattern, as well as the rough texture of the brickwork.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern, with each floor featuring evenly spaced rectangular windows aligned vertically and horizontally.
